penalties.description: The cut deviated from the marked circle at several points.

consensus_score.reasoning: Both models agreed on completion time and detected line deviations. Claude detected circle_release phase while GPT did not, but both confirmed task completion. Penalty deduction amount differed between models - using GPT's more conservative 5-point deduction as both models agreed deviations occurred but exact severity assessment varied.

disagreements.claude_value: Visible cutting deviations from the marked circle line, particularly in the final quarter of the circle.
disagreements.gpt_value: The cut deviated from the marked circle at several points.
disagreements.rationale: GPT's description was more general and both models agreed deviations occurred, so the more conservative description was retained.
Both models detected line deviations but disagreed on severity. Without clear visual evidence to support the higher penalty, the more conservative 5-point deduction was applied.
Claude's detection of circle_release is consistent with task completion confirmation from both models, so the more complete phase detection was retained.
